snippets.title: Satori Card Renderer (Edge API Route)
snippets.description: Next.js API route that renders JSX card templates to PNG via @vercel/og ImageResponse on Vercel Edge Runtime.
gotchas: Satori supports flexbox only — no CSS Grid, no complex gradients, no blend modes
All styles must be inline objects, not className or CSS modules
Custom fonts must be loaded as ArrayBuffer at request time
ImageResponse constructor limits: max ~50KB of JSX per render

snippets.title: Centralized Design Tokens
snippets.description: Template colour/font/spacing tokens in a single config file, consumed by all card templates and (future) UI components.
gotchas: Tokens are typed as Record<TemplateName, TemplateTokens> — adding a new template requires updating the TemplateName union first
Font names in tokens must match fonts actually loaded in the satori renderer

snippets.title: Satori-Compatible Card Template (Klassisch)
snippets.description: React component that renders a styled catch card using only satori-compatible CSS (flexbox, inline styles). 55% photo / 45% content split with optional info blocks.
gotchas: Every <div> needs display:'flex' — satori does not default to block layout
Images must be absolute URLs or data URIs — no relative paths
Ecology substitute detection uses text.startsWith('🌿') — fragile if emoji prefix changes

snippets.title: Query Param Card Block Parser
snippets.description: Parses comma-separated block names from URL query params into a typed CardBlockSelection object. All blocks default to false (opt-in).
gotchas: Block names must match CardBlockSelection keys exactly — no fuzzy matching
Returns all-false if blocksParam is null or empty (safe default)

snippets.title: Custom CSV Parser (No Dependencies)
snippets.description: Hand-written CSV parser that handles quoted fields with embedded commas. Replaces csv-parse to keep dependency count low (Rule 11).
gotchas: Does not handle escaped quotes within quoted fields (\")
Assumes UTF-8 encoding
Good enough for seed data — use a proper library for user-uploaded CSVs

snippets.title: Batch Card Generator Script
snippets.description: Reads species CSV, iterates species × templates × formats, hits the card API for each combination, and saves PNGs to disk. Used for smoke test batch generation.
gotchas: Requires dev server running in a separate terminal (http://localhost:3000)
Generates 18 species × 3 templates × 2 formats = 108 cards — takes a few minutes
No retry logic — if one card fails, the script continues but logs the error

snippets.title: i18n String Table (German-First)
snippets.description: Flat key-value string table with dot-separated keys and typed I18nKey export. All UI strings in German, code comments in English.
gotchas: Keys must match across all locale files when translations are added
No interpolation support yet — add {placeholder} syntax when needed
I18nKey type is derived via keyof typeof — adding keys auto-updates the type

snippets.title: MMKV Offline Request Queue Pattern
snippets.description: Custom offline queue using MMKV key-value storage. Enqueues pending operations when offline, processes on reconnect via NetInfo listener.
gotchas: MMKV stores strings — must JSON.stringify/parse objects
No built-in retry backoff — add exponential backoff for production
Queue items persist across app restarts (good for offline-first, but clear stale items)

snippets.title: EXIF GPS + Timestamp Extraction
snippets.description: Selective EXIF field extraction using exifr — pulls only GPS and DateTime to minimise parsing overhead on mobile.
gotchas: exifr returns undefined for missing fields — check before using
GPS coordinates need conversion from DMS to decimal degrees in some cases
expo-image-manipulator strips EXIF — extract BEFORE processing

snippets.title: View-Shot Card Capture (React Native)
snippets.description: Capture a React Native View hierarchy as PNG using react-native-view-shot, then share via react-native-share.
gotchas: Component must be rendered (on-screen or in a hidden view) to capture
Capture resolution depends on device pixel ratio — set explicit dimensions
react-native-share can target specific apps (Instagram, WhatsApp) but API varies by platform
